content: Language learning's deliberate reflection practice (from the vocabulary learning context of the spelling variant "reflexion") can be adapted as a structured curriculum for improving LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where LLMs learn to refine their outputs the same way human students refine their writing and critical thinking through guided reflection. This framework treats LLM self-reflection can be scaffolded through incremental, vocabulary-sized repeated reflection exercises, just like high school students master new concepts through iterative reflective writing practice.
